answer: Three of the UDR's influence-data subscription handlers keep running after sending a 404, and the ueId format check used elsewhere in the file is written so that it always succeeds, leaving both the routing guard and the identifier validation effectively non-functional.
reasoning:
### Vulnerability class
Broken access-control guard (missing return after an error response) plus an ineffective input-validation regex.

### Location — issue 1
`HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete`, `HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdGet`, and `HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdPut` all check `influenceId != "subs-to-notify"` and, on mismatch, call `c.String(http.StatusNotFound, "404 page not found")` — but none of the three handlers actually stops there. In Gin, writing a response body does not by itself abort the function, so execution falls through to read `subscriptionId` and invoke the corresponding processor procedure regardless of whether the guard condition was true.

### Location — issue 2
`HandleCreateEeSubscriptions` (and `HandleQueryeesubscriptions`, which follows the same pattern) validates `ueId` with the regular expression `^(imsi-[0-9]{5,15}|nai-.+|msisdn-[0-9]{5,15}|extid-[^@]+@[^@]+|gci-.+|gli-.+|.+)$`. The final alternative, `.+`, matches any non-empty string on its own, which means the whole alternation is satisfied by anything that is not empty — the preceding, more specific alternatives are dead code and the check adds nothing beyond the existing `ueId == ""` guard.

### Impact in the 5G core
For issue 1, a request whose `influenceId` path segment is anything other than the literal `subs-to-notify` still reaches the DELETE/GET/PUT processor with whatever `subscriptionId` was supplied, and the client may additionally receive two overlapping HTTP writes — undefined wire behavior that a well-behaved SBI peer would not expect. For issue 2, since the UDR is the authoritative persistence layer for subscriber data across the whole 5G core, any string reaching `CreateEeSubscriptionsProcedure` as `ueId` skips real format enforcement entirely, so the format guarantees other NFs (and MongoDB query construction) may implicitly rely on are not actually being provided by this endpoint.

### Fix
Add an explicit `return` immediately after each `c.String(http.StatusNotFound, ...)` call in the three subs-to-notify handlers so the function halts on the rejected path. For the regex, delete the trailing `|.+` alternative and enumerate only the accepted identifier formats (or reuse a shared validator such as the SUPI/GPSI validation already used by other NFs) so that an unmatched identifier is genuinely rejected.
